answer: The proper times for ritual bathing are enumerated in Viṣṇu Smṛti, Chapter 64 (work slug: unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8), verses Vi_64.6–Vi_64.9. The text states: not at night (Vi_64.6); except when an eclipse is visible (Vi_64.7); not at the twilights (Vi_64.8); and one who is accustomed to bathing in the morning shall bathe after seeing the eastern sky become reddish-tawny (Vi_64.9). Together, these verses enumerate the proper times by prohibition and positive prescription: daytime (not night), not at twilight, morning when the eastern sky reddens, with the special exception that bathing is permitted at night during an eclipse.
